topic.what: Gaussian and Gaussian mixture mechanisms for Rényi Pufferfish Privacy under correlated data
topic.how: Derive exact Rényi divergence for Gaussian perturbation, closed-form sufficient conditions, optimal-transport-based GMM calibration
topic.why_matters: Addresses conservative noise injection in existing RPP mechanisms, improves privacy-utility tradeoff for correlated data

characteristics.what_it_measures: Required noise variance reduction vs baseline
characteristics.evidence: Average noise reduction across UCI datasets (Adult, Heart Disease, Student Performance)
characteristics.context: Statistical and model-output queries (RAW, MEAN, BNN, GP)

characteristics.vs_baseline: Smaller than baseline across all epsilon values
characteristics.evidence: Figures 5-7 show systematic downward shift vs Pierquin et al.
characteristics.context: Monotonically decreasing with epsilon, monotonicity with alpha depends on mean-variance dominance

characteristics.value: O(1) closed-form vs O(log(1/tolerance)) binary search
characteristics.context: Closed-form sufficient condition eliminates iterative computation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Noise variance behavior vs privacy budget
characteristics.evidence: Proposition III.6 proof and empirical validation in Figures 5-7
characteristics.context: Verified across all query types and datasets

characteristics.what_it_measures: Noise variance behavior vs Renyi order
characteristics.evidence: Proposition III.7: increasing if mean dominates, decreasing if variance dominates

characteristics.vs_baseline: Consistently lower than Pierquin et al.
characteristics.context: Varies by dataset, query type, and epsilon
